mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP directly increases corporate earnings expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as valuation multiples expand alongside fundamental improvements.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as discount rates dominate cash flow projections.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and a decline in total nonfarm payrolls signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate revenue contractions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of observed instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and stronger aggregate supply, which reduces input cost pressures and supports corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no significant positive correlation with month-over-month INDPRO changes, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 55 percent of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1500 thousand units indicates weakening consumer confidence and reduced wealth effects, which suppresses discretionary spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 60 percent of months where HOUST drops below 1500, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.01 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS remains flat, real interest rates decline, triggering inflation hedging flows into equities that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investors seek nominal asset protection.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when the CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS ratio exceeds its historical median, or the interaction effect reverses sign in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer credit balances indicate expanding household borrowing capacity and strong aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly higher when RSAFS expands month-over-month, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 55 percent of expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals the onset of a labor market recession, which historically precedes equity drawdowns and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over three months, or the historical hit rate drops below 45 percent in walk-forward testing.
